name: Cardinal Credit Union, INC.
description_short: Member-owned credit union serving Ohio with branches in Mentor, Canfield, Mayfield Heights, and Ashtabula. Offers checking, savings, loans, CDs, and VISA rewards cards.
description_long:
Cardinal Credit Union is a not-for-profit, member-owned financial cooperative operating in northeastern Ohio with multiple branch locations. The organization emphasizes its "People Helping People" philosophy and operates under NCUA insurance protections typical of federal credit unions. The institution has established partnerships with the Cleveland Browns organization, sponsoring youth fan programs and financial literacy initiatives for high school students.

Cardinal's service offerings include share accounts (checking/savings), certificate of deposit products, auto loans, home equity loans, personal loans, bill consolidation loans, college tuition loans, and VISA credit card products. Members can access online banking, payment centers, ATM networks through shared branching, and appointment-based in-branch services. The credit union actively promotes financial literacy through branded debit card programs and educational materials for younger members.

The institution distinguishes itself through competitive rewards programs: a 1.5% cash back rewards VISA with no earning limits and a Signature Rewards card offering 1.5 points per dollar spent with redemption options for merchandise, cash, or travel. Cardinal also features promotional CD rates (advertised at 4.50% APY for 7-month CDs with $5,000 minimum) and maintains consistent branch hours across locations with Saturday availability. The credit union's youth-focused initiatives, including Browns partnership programs and high school savings accounts, differentiate it in member engagement.

As a credit union, Cardinal operates on a cooperative model benefiting member-owners rather than external shareholders. However, membership eligibility requirements, service limitations compared to larger institutions, and reduced physical branch density compared to major banks may present considerations for some consumers. The organization appears stable and operationally sound based on published service offerings and community partnerships.
services: Certificate of Deposit (CD) products with promotional rates
VISA credit cards with cash back and signature rewards
Online banking enrollment and account management
ATM access and shared branching network
pros: 1.5% cash back on all VISA purchases with no earning limits
Competitive CD rates advertised at 4.50% APY (7-month terms)
Multiple branch locations in Ohio with Saturday hours
Access to shared branching network beyond owned branches
Comprehensive loan products including auto, home equity, personal, and educational loans
Strong community engagement through Cleveland Browns youth partnerships and financial literacy programs
NCUA insurance protection for member deposits
cons: Limited geographic footprint (northeast Ohio only with 4 named branches)
Branches closed on Wednesdays and Sundays, limiting accessibility
No mention of mobile app or advanced digital banking features on homepage
Limited online content regarding eligibility requirements, fees, or detailed product terms
Smaller institution may have fewer resources than larger credit unions or banks for specialized services
best_for: Ohio residents in Cuyahoga, Mahoning, and surrounding counties seeking credit union membership
Families interested in youth financial literacy programs and youth-focused banking products
Customers prioritizing VISA rewards earning on everyday purchases
Borrowers seeking relationship-based lending with local community institution
diagnosis: Cardinal Credit Union is best for Ohio residents seeking membership in a not-for-profit financial cooperative with local branch access, competitive VISA rewards, and community-focused banking. Primary caveat: geographic limitation to northeast Ohio and limited public information about membership eligibility, fee structures, and digital banking capabilities.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.

meta_description: Cardinal Credit Union, Inc. — NCUA-insured credit union in Mentor, OH. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
